label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the paragraph carefully. So, Mr. Williams is a careful driver who encountered a crossroads with a "SLOW" sign. He slowed down, looked both ways, and then drove across without stopping completely. Then a policeman stopped him, saying he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams explained he saw the "SLOW" sign and just went slow. The policeman realized he was in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The answer choices are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly From the story, when Mr. Williams approached the crossroads with a "SLOW" sign, he slowed down. So he was driving slowly at that point. Then he drove across without stopping. The policeman stopped him because he didn't stop, but Mr. Williams argues that the sign said "SLOW" not "STOP". The key here is the sequence of events. When the policeman approached him, Mr. Williams had already passed the crossroads by slowing down and not stopping. The policeman's realization that he was in the wrong street suggests maybe the sign was actually a "STOP" sign, but the story states the sign said "SLOW". So, when the policeman stopped Mr. Williams, he was already past the crossroads and was in the act of driving across the main road, which he had already slowed for and proceeded without stopping.
